jokes.text: His new thing... Piggyback rides. Anytime you want. Piggyback! Piggyback! Move! Piggyback. Bam!
jokes.setup: Tom explaining Andy's fun new workplace behavior
jokes.punchline: The enthusiastic, childlike execution of piggyback rides in a government office
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Andy's childlike enthusiasm contrasted with workplace setting. Physical comedy inferred from dialogue.

jokes.text: Giddyup!
jokes.setup: Andy giving piggyback rides
jokes.punchline: Horse sound effect while giving human piggyback rides
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment. Great economy with single word.

jokes.text: The only trails he's gonna be surveying are trails of lies and deception.
jokes.setup: Ron claiming they're doing a trail survey
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on 'trails' meaning both hiking paths and evidence of dishonesty
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels. Tom's dramatic delivery style fits perfectly.

jokes.text: Not all the guys. He's never taken me. Fine, all the men.
jokes.setup: Tom correcting himself about Ron taking 'all the guys'
jokes.punchline: Awkward admission that he's excluded, then defensive correction to 'men'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's insecurity and need to maintain dignity.

jokes.text: Is this not rap?
jokes.setup: Leslie pointing out Ron is listening to turkey calls
jokes.punchline: Tom genuinely confused, thinking turkey calls might be rap music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - his urban sensibility clashing with outdoor activities. Highly quotable and specific.

jokes.text: And just like that, the one tiny aspect of government I enjoyed was clubbed to death before my eyes.
jokes.setup: Leslie insisting on joining the hunting trip
jokes.punchline: Ron's dramatic metaphor about his joy being 'clubbed to death'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron voice - libertarian who hates government finding the one thing he enjoyed being ruined. Strong metaphor.

jokes.text: You want me to dial a number, and then read another number out loud? Can you handle this?
jokes.setup: Ron asking if April can handle a simple phone task
jokes.punchline: April breaking down the task to show how absurdly simple it is
jokes.explanation: Classic April - turning Ron's condescension back on him with perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Cool.
jokes.setup: Ron saying he'll name the first turkey after her if she does the task
jokes.punchline: April's completely flat, unimpressed response to this 'honor'
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan. Maximum economy - one word carries the entire joke about her disinterest.

jokes.text: Gross.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing Ann her official Indiana hunting license
jokes.punchline: Ann's disgusted reaction to being made an official hunter
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Ann's aversion to hunting. Basic but effective.

jokes.text: Holy cow. It is good to be back! Sneak attack! Damn it! I'm the pants king! Bow to me.
jokes.setup: The guys arriving at the hunting cabin
jokes.punchline: Immediate pantsing and Jerry declaring himself 'pants king'
jokes.explanation: Jerry getting a rare moment of dominance through pantsing. Physical comedy with character reversal.

jokes.text: I'm the pants queen! What the hell! Bow to the pants queen.
jokes.setup: Leslie joining the pantsing tradition
jokes.punchline: Leslie successfully pantsing someone and claiming queenship
jokes.explanation: Leslie proving she can hang with the boys by immediately succeeding at their game.

jokes.text: When you're out with the boys, you gotta be ready for a good pantsing. That's why I have suspenders that connect my bra to my jeans.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining hunting trip preparation
jokes.punchline: Revealing her elaborate anti-pantsing contraption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Leslie's over-preparation and commitment to being 'one of the guys.' Highly original and quotable.

jokes.text: I would not have pegged you as a user of mouth tobacco. I'm full of surprises, Ron.
jokes.setup: Ron offering chewing tobacco and Tom accepting
jokes.punchline: Tom's suave response about being 'full of surprises'
jokes.explanation: Tom trying to seem rugged and surprising Ron, setting up his inevitable failure.

jokes.text: I swallowed it. You're supposed to swallow it?
jokes.setup: Tom feeling sick from chewing tobacco
jokes.punchline: Revealing he swallowed it and genuinely thought that was correct
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom moment - urban guy failing at rural activities despite trying to be suave. Great setup/payoff.

jokes.text: That's why they call it chew and not swallow. Am I right, Ron? Yes, you are right.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining the obvious about chewing tobacco
jokes.punchline: Ron's deadpan confirmation of this painfully obvious statement
jokes.explanation: Leslie stating the obvious with Ron's patient confirmation. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: The only way to defeat the beast is to find the beast within.
jokes.setup: Group doing traditional hunting toast
jokes.punchline: Tom's overly philosophical and dramatic hunting wisdom
jokes.explanation: Tom trying to sound deep and mystical about hunting. Fits his character's pretensions perfectly.

jokes.text: Ron, your toast sucked. The traditional toast is 'to the hunt.' And it is said by me.
jokes.setup: Tom giving an elaborate hunting toast
jokes.punchline: Jerry asserting his authority over hunting trip traditions
jokes.explanation: Jerry showing rare assertiveness about hunting traditions. Good character moment.

jokes.text: I always forget because you're so pretty you're not used to rejection.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggesting mixed doubles hunting
jokes.punchline: Mark's backhanded compliment about Leslie being too pretty to understand rejection
jokes.explanation: Mark's awkward attempt at flattery while rejecting Leslie's suggestion. Solid character work.

jokes.text: I've been on hold for an hour. I really have to pee. Can you sit here and just listen?
jokes.setup: April assigned to call the state parks office
jokes.punchline: Her completely casual request for Andy to take over so she can pee
jokes.explanation: April's matter-of-fact approach to shirking responsibility. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: I know this weekend you were looking forward to a lot of man on man on man action
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to be understanding about Ron's hunting trip
jokes.punchline: Her innocent but unfortunate phrasing about 'man on man on man action'
jokes.explanation: Leslie's complete innocence creating accidental innuendo. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: What the***? What the hell? Give me some warning. I saw a quail. Sorry, man. You snooze, you loose.
jokes.setup: Tom shooting without warning while hunting with Ron
jokes.punchline: Tom's casual 'you snooze you lose' attitude about hunting safety
jokes.explanation: Tom's competitive nature overriding basic hunting safety. Builds toward his eventual accident.

jokes.text: Guys love it when you can show them you're better than they are at something they love.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her bonding strategy with Ron
jokes.punchline: Her complete misunderstanding of male psychology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie logic - completely wrong but delivered with total confidence. Great dramatic irony.

jokes.text: I am on hold with the State Parks Department I am on hold so suck on my butt
jokes.setup: Andy taking over April's phone duty
jokes.punchline: Andy making up a song about being on hold with childish lyrics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy - turning mundane task into silly entertainment. Childlike creativity fits character perfectly.

jokes.text: Maybe a deer will eat him. That'd be awesome, but I don't think that will happen, probably.
jokes.setup: Andy expressing jealousy about Mark being with Ann
jokes.punchline: Andy's hopeful but realistic assessment of Mark being eaten by a deer
jokes.explanation: Andy's innocent malice and childlike logic about wildlife behavior. Great character voice.

jokes.text: You surprised that my breasts didn't throw my aim off?
jokes.setup: Leslie successfully hunting
jokes.punchline: Leslie's awkward attempt to address gender stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Leslie's awkward way of proving herself leads to uncomfortable oversharing. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Your favorite cake can't be birthday cake. That's like saying, your favorite kind of cereal is breakfast cereal. I love breakfast cereal.
jokes.setup: Ann and Tom discussing cake preferences
jokes.punchline: Tom's illogical analogy followed by completely missing the point
jokes.explanation: Tom's faulty logic and Ann's simple response. Good character interplay showing their different mindsets.

jokes.text: Look, some kind of bird. Let's kill it. You talkin' to me, bitch?
jokes.setup: Tom spotting a bird while hunting
jokes.punchline: Tom's gangster-like trash talk to a bird before shooting
jokes.explanation: Tom applying his urban attitude to hunting, treating a bird like a street adversary. Good character specificity.

jokes.text: See, at my house I've got a wife and three beautiful daughters. But this trip, it is the one time of year I get to pee standing up.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining why the hunting trip is special
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's not allowed to pee standing up at home
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from sweet family man to emasculated husband. Perfect Jerry character work with great structure.

jokes.text: I've been shot! Somebody shot me in the head!
jokes.setup: Hunting trip proceeding normally
jokes.punchline: Sudden shocking announcement that Ron has been shot
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation moment that shifts the entire episode's stakes. Great structure and impact.

jokes.text: Ron, it's not that serious. I just need you to stay calm. I'm just gonna stay angry. I find that relaxes me.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to calm injured Ron
jokes.punchline: Ron's contrarian approach to stress management
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron philosophy - anger as relaxation technique. Great character consistency even while injured.

jokes.text: There was a bird kind of near me, and I know you want to prove yourself. No, I swear I didn't. I swear to God, I've never shot anyone.
jokes.setup: Ron asking if Leslie shot him
jokes.punchline: Leslie's defensive overcompensation about never shooting anyone
jokes.explanation: Leslie's guilty overreaction makes her seem more suspicious. Good character-driven suspicion building.

jokes.text: When I look at my palm, I see a lady's mouth french kissing a dog. Is that normal?
jokes.setup: Ann checking on medicated Ron's condition
jokes.punchline: Ron's specific and disturbing hallucination
jokes.explanation: Highly original and specific hallucination. The bizarre specificity makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Seve... Eight. But I washed 'em down with plenty of fluids. You cannot drink scotch with this! You're gonna need to purge right now, okay? No, I'm not wasting 20-year scotch.
jokes.setup: Discovering Ron took too many pain pills
jokes.punchline: Ron's priorities: refusing to waste good scotch even while overdosing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - prioritizing expensive scotch over his own health. Great escalation.

jokes.text: On a scale of one to Chris Brown, how pissed is he?
jokes.setup: Tom asking about Ron's anger level
jokes.punchline: Using Chris Brown as the anger measurement scale
jokes.explanation: Dark topical reference that's very much Tom's voice. Edgy but fits the character's pop culture obsession.

jokes.text: Maybe Ron shot himself. He has seemed really depressed lately. He was shot in the back of the head. Right, he loves the back of his head. He would never shoot himself there.
jokes.setup: Tom suggesting Ron shot himself
jokes.punchline: Jerry's illogical reasoning about Ron's emotional attachment to parts of his head
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jerry logic - the idea that someone would have sentimental attachment to parts of their own head. Absurd but perfectly Jerry.

jokes.text: Man is the most dangerous game. To the predator. I did smell something out there, and it wasn't human. That was pine trees.
jokes.setup: Tom making dramatic statements about hunting predators
jokes.punchline: Mark deflating Tom's drama by identifying pine trees
jokes.explanation: Tom's overactive imagination vs Mark's practical reality. Good contrast between characters.

jokes.text: Don't shoot! It's Craig from Reinhold Mercedes! Craig, I got you, dawg! Don't worry, I'm comin'!
jokes.setup: Group thinking there's a dangerous predator outside
jokes.punchline: It's just a car salesman, and Tom knows him personally
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - building tension then revealing it's just Tom's car salesman. Perfect absurdist deflation.

jokes.text: That man wasn't my brother. He was my husband! I'm pregnant... With Josh Groban's baby!
jokes.setup: Drugged Ron practicing dramatic line readings
jokes.punchline: Ron delivering soap opera-style revelations while completely out of it
jokes.explanation: Ron completely out of character due to drugs, delivering melodramatic soap opera lines. Good contrast with his usual stoicism.

jokes.text: What do you mean the squirrel... took the nuts out of the... Out of that kid's backpack... And ate 'em? That is a fact. A fact about me.
jokes.setup: Ron trying to practice spitting due to drug effects
jokes.punchline: Ron's complete inability to form coherent thoughts or reasons to spit
jokes.explanation: Ron's drug-addled attempts at conversation. Funny but not as strong as other drugged Ron moments.

jokes.text: I am. I shot Ron Swanson. You shot my Mercedes? What? No. No!
jokes.setup: Leslie confessing to shooting Ron
jokes.punchline: Craig mishearing and thinking she shot his Mercedes
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with Craig's priorities. Simple but effective mishearing gag.

jokes.text: I find a lot of women have problems with tunnel vision.
jokes.setup: Leslie confessing to shooting Ron
jokes.punchline: Ranger's casual sexism while taking her statement
jokes.explanation: Straightforward sexist ranger character moment. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: I let my emotions get the best of me. I just... I cared too much, I guess. I was thinking with my lady parts. I was walking, and I felt something icky. I thought there was gonna be chocolate. I don't even remember. I'm wearing a new bra, and it closes in the front. So it popped open and it threw me off. All I want to do is have babies.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to give a believable confession
jokes.punchline: Leslie spiraling into increasingly absurd feminine stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Leslie playing into sexist stereotypes to protect Tom. Each excuse gets more ridiculous. Great character work.

jokes.text: This would not happen if I had a penis! What? Bitches be crazy. I'm good at tolerating pain, I'm bad at math, and I'm stupid.
jokes.setup: Leslie continuing her absurd confession
jokes.punchline: Leslie's final desperate attempt to embody every sexist stereotype
jokes.explanation: Perfect climax to Leslie's stereotype spiral. The penis comment and 'bitches be crazy' are peak absurdity. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You know, if I gave you a hickey, it would totally make Ann jealous. I don't know, I think that would... That's pretty gross. What's weird about one friend sucking on another friend's neck?
jokes.setup: April suggesting a hickey to make Ann jealous
jokes.punchline: April's matter-of-fact description of hickey-giving between friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan manipulation. Her casual description of something intimate is very her character.

jokes.text: I gave my gay boyfriend's boyfriend a hickey, and it totally made my gay boyfriend jealous.
jokes.setup: April explaining why the hickey plan will work
jokes.punchline: April's complicated relationship history involving her gay boyfriend's boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April backstory reveal. The convoluted gay relationship triangle is perfectly her weird life. Very quotable.

jokes.text: You know, Leslie, the Super Bowl's in a couple months. I usually watch it with my brothers. Maybe you could come by at halftime and shoot me in the head.
jokes.setup: Ron still angry about being shot
jokes.punchline: Ron's specific sarcastic invitation involving the Super Bowl
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure with specific Super Bowl reference. Ron's sustained anger expressed through detailed sarcasm.

jokes.text: Or perhaps next time I'm enjoying some alone time in the men's restroom, you could invite yourself into my stall and shoot me in the head.
jokes.setup: Ron continuing his sarcastic rant
jokes.punchline: Ron's bathroom-specific scenario for being shot
jokes.explanation: Part of the callback series but not as strong as others. The bathroom setting adds specificity but less impact.

jokes.text: Maybe the next time I'm at the doctor's office getting my prostate examined, you could come by and shoot me in the head.
jokes.setup: Ron interrupting to continue his rant
jokes.punchline: Ron's most intimate and uncomfortable scenario for being shot
jokes.explanation: Good escalation in the callback series - prostate exam is the most vulnerable scenario. Solid structure.

jokes.text: You did good. You're a real stand-up guy. I'm sorry I lost my temper. It was because I was shot in the head by a moron.
jokes.setup: Ron acknowledging Leslie protected Tom
jokes.punchline: Ron's backhanded apology that still insults Tom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - showing respect for Leslie while still insulting Tom. Good balance of appreciation and disdain.

jokes.text: Pants king. Pants queen.
jokes.setup: Episode ending with reconciliation
jokes.punchline: Return to the innocent pantsing rivalry from the beginning
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure bringing the episode full circle. Great economy and structure, returning to innocent fun after the chaos.
